journal_entry.rationale: When the customer deposit was originally received, it was recorded as a liability (Deferred Revenue / Customer Deposits) because the service had not yet been performed. No cash changes hands and no tax lines are required — the tax would have been handled at the time the deposit was collected.

The Entry Builder then tried to force-fit the erroneous two-credit-line tuple by inventing an HST Payable line, assuming an Ontario 13% HST tax-inclusive deposit with no basis in the transaction description. The HST Payable line is fabricated, and the $88.50/$11.50 split is unsupported.

output_credit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill means the expense has been incurred but not yet paid. The credit side records an Accounts Payable (or accrued liability) — a liability increase. No cash or asset has left yet.

output_debit_corrector.reason: Accruing a utility bill is correctly recorded as an expense increase (slot c). The debit tuple is already correct. Note: the credit tuple showing an asset increase appears to be a credit-side error — accrued expenses should credit a liability — but that is outside the scope of debit correction.

output_credit_corrector.reason: Accruing a utility bill creates a liability (Accrued Expenses/Accounts Payable), so a liability increase in slot (a) is correct. The debit tuple incorrectly shows revenue increase instead of expense increase, but that is a debit-side error outside this agent's scope. The credit classification is semantically sound as-is.

output_entry_builder.rationale: The debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one expense line — Utilities Expense is debited to recognize the cost in the current period. The credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability line — Accrued Liabilities (or Accounts Payable) is credited since the bill has not yet been paid, creating an obligation. No tax information was provided, so no tax lines are added. Total debits ($50) = total credits ($50).

output_approver.reason: Entry correctly records a $50 utility bill accrual. Utilities Expense is debited (expense increase, correct debit side) and Accrued Liabilities is credited (liability increase, correct credit side). Total debits ($50) = total credits ($50) — balanced. No tax lines are needed as no tax information was provided. Accounts are appropriate for a standard accrual entry.
